input.command:
python3 -c "
import json
# Search Feb 27 articles for war details
with open('articles/2026/02/27/articles.jsonl') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()
print(f'Feb 27 articles: {len(lines)}')
results = []
for line in lines:
    a = json.loads(line)
    title = (a.get('title','') or '').lower()
    content = (a.get('content','') or '').lower()[:3000]
    if ('iran' in title or 'tehran' in title) and ('strike' in content[:500] or 'bomb' in content[:500] or 'attack' in content[:500] or 'war' in content[:500]):
        results.append(((a.get('title','') or '')[:120], (a.get('content','') or '')[:500]))
    elif ('israel' in title and ('iran' in content[:500] or 'hezbollah' in content[:500]) and ('strike' in content[:500] or 'attack' in content[:500])):
        results.append(((a.get('title','') or '')[:120], (a.get('content','') or '')[:500]))
    elif 'hezbollah' in title and ('attack' in content[:500] or 'missile' in content[:500] or 'rocket' in content[:500]):
        results.append(((a.get('title','') or '')[:120], (a.get('content','') or '')[:500]))
for t,c in results[:15]:
    print(t)
    print(f'  {c[:400]}')
    print()
" 2>/dev/null

python3 -c "
import json
# Search for specific military targets, hospitals, governing council
with open('articles/2026/02/27/articles.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
        a = json.loads(line)
        content = (a.get('content','') or '').lower()[:5000]
        title = (a.get('title','') or '').lower()
        # Hospital in Tehran
        if 'hospital' in content and 'tehran' in content and ('strike' in content or 'bomb' in content or 'hit' in content):
            print(f'[HOSPITAL] {(a.get(\"title\",\"\") or \"\")[:120]}')
            print(f'  {content[:500]}')
            print('===')
        # Governing council
        elif 'governing council' in content or ('interim' in content and 'council' in content and 'iran' in content):
            print(f'[COUNCIL] {(a.get(\"title\",\"\") or \"\")[:120]}')
            print(f'  {content[:500]}')
            print('===')
        # Hezbollah attack
        elif 'hezbollah' in content and ('avenging' in content or 'retaliation' in content or 'nasrallah' in content):
            if 'attack' in content or 'rocket' in content or 'missile' in content:
                print(f'[HEZB] {(a.get(\"title\",\"\") or \"\")[:120]}')
                print(f'  {content[:500]}')
                print('===')
" 2>/dev/null | head -40

blocks.content:
IPL 2026 likely to be played from March 28 to May 31
The Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026 season will start on March 28, two days after what the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) had originally planned, according to ESPNcricinfo.
In December 2025, the IPL had informed all the franchises that the 2026 edition would start on March 26, but according to ESPNcricinfo reports, the IPL has changed the start date to March 28, with the grand finale to be played on May 31.
Stay up to date with the latest news. Follow KT on WhatsApp Channels
The report further added that the Governing Council of IPL will meet next week to finalise the schedule f
===
BCCI changes plans for IPL 2026, start of tournament set to be pushed back
"Yes, there’s been a change in plans. The IPL 2026 season is now most likely to begin two days later, on March 28. There is no change to the date for the final; it will go ahead on May 31,” a source confirmed to Hindustan Times Digital.
Just one month remains before the cash-rich league gets underway, but the schedule has yet to be announced. It is now common knowledge that the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) was waiting for the Election Commission to announce the dates for the assembly elections in Assam, West Bengal and Tamil Nadu.
Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR), Chennai Super King
===
